title: Deliver a Stump Speech
description: Write and deliver a 7-minute stump speech — the core campaign speech that introduces you, your values, and your platform to voters. Record, review, and refine until it is natural and compelling.
whyItMatters: Every politician needs a stump speech — the speech they can deliver at any community event, door-knock, or media opportunity. It is your personal pitch to voters. If you cannot deliver it confidently in seven minutes, you are not ready to run.

steps.title: Define your story
steps.description: Write your personal story — why are you running, what shaped your values, what connects you to this community?
steps.tips: Be specific and authentic. 'I grew up in a small town where the local hospital closed' is better than 'I care about healthcare'.

steps.title: Draft the speech
steps.description: Structure: personal story (90 seconds), the problem (90 seconds), your solution (2 minutes), vision and call to action (2 minutes).
steps.tips: Write it to be spoken, not read. Short sentences. Active voice. Conversational tone.

steps.title: Rehearse aloud
steps.description: Deliver the speech aloud at least 10 times. Time yourself. Cut anything that feels forced or goes over 7 minutes.
steps.tips: Rehearse standing, with gestures, as if a room of 50 people is watching.

steps.title: Record and review
steps.description: Record yourself on video delivering the speech. Watch it once for content, once for body language, once for vocal delivery.
steps.tips: Focus on eye contact (imagine faces), pacing (slow down), and filler words (um, ah, like).

steps.title: Get feedback
steps.description: Deliver the speech to three people — a friend, someone in your target demographic, and someone who disagrees with you politically.
steps.tips: Ask each person: 'What did you remember?' The answer reveals your most and least effective sections.

steps.title: Final version
steps.description: Revise based on feedback. Record the final version. You should be able to deliver it without notes.
steps.tips: The goal is not perfection — it is authenticity with structure.

deliverables: A video recording of the final delivery
A one-page self-assessment noting strengths and areas for improvement

rubric.description: Does the speech establish an authentic personal connection with the audience?
Is the problem clearly defined and the solution specific and credible?
Is the delivery confident, natural, and engaging — not robotic or over-rehearsed?
Is the speech within the 7-minute limit with appropriate pacing?
Can a listener recall the key message after hearing it once?
